subclass.name: Path of the Broken Oath
subclassFeatures: Path of the Broken Oath|Barbarian|PHB|Broken Oath|SMRBarb|3

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Bloodline
entries: The Path of the Bloodline is rarely chosen — most barbarians who walk this path were always destined to. Magic pulses in their blood, due to a mighty ancestor or some other quirk of heritage. In a different individual, this might manifest as sorcerous powers — but a Bloodline barbarian instead learns to access it through their primal rage.

Bloodline barbarians may come from any background, though something about their lineage is often worthy of note. Similarly, they may come from any alignment — their ancestors' character has no bearing on how they choose to use their power.

entries.name: Path of the Bloodline Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: Your eyes glow with sorcerous fire when you rage.
Your were born with mystical tattoos, which glow when you cast spells.
According to family stories, you look exactly like one particular ancestor.
You have a bad habit of attempting to cast a spell while not raging. This fails, makes you completely furious, and then you rage.

subclassFeature.name: Sorcerous Bloodline
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, your magical inheritance begins to express itself. Select {@filter one bloodline from the {@homebrew included options|list at the end of this document}|optionalfeatures|source=|feature type=sorcerousbloodline|miscellaneous=}.

Additionally, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill, and you learn the spell {@spell detect magic}, with Charisma as your spellcasting ability. You can cast it a number of times per day equal to your proficiency modifier, regaining all uses after a long rest.

entries.name: Sorcerous Origins
entries: As an alternative to the {@homebrew included options|list below}, select the origin spell list from any {@class sorcerer} subclass which has one, and work with your DM to choose an appropriate damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Blood Rage
entries: Also at 3rd level, your rage enables you to unleash your bloodline's power. As you gain levels, you learn the spells associated with your bloodline, as shown on the bloodline's spells table. You can only cast them while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, and casting any of your bloodline spells counts as dealing damage for purposes of maintaining your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1|}. You may maintain concentration on your bloodline spells while {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}.
You may cast one spell of each spell level (1st-5th), regaining all casts after completing a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Charisma is your spellcasting ability for your bloodline spells. You use your Charisma whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Charisma modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a bloodline spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

subclassFeature.name: Shield of Sorcery
entries: Also at 3rd level, while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you can add your Charisma modifier to your armor class in place of your Dexterity or Constitution modifier (your choice) when you use your {@classFeature Unarmored Defense|Barbarian||1}.

subclassFeature.name: Inherited Defenses
entries: At 6th level, your bloodline gives you tremendous resilience. When you begin {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you gain temporary hit points equal to your Charisma modifier + half your barbarian level. Additionally, while you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you gain resistance to the damage type specified by your Sorcerous Bloodline.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Assault
entries: At 10th level, you gain greater mastery of your bloodline's power. When you cast a bloodline spell, you may make one weapon attack as a bonus action.
Additionally, when you cast your bloodline spells, you may add half your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) to your spell save DC and spell attack modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodline Transformation
entries: At 14th level, your inborn power finds full expression in your rage. While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you gain the following additional benefits:

items: Your weapon attacks deal {@damage 1d6} bonus damage of the type specified by your Sorcerous Bloodline.
When you cast one of your bloodline spells, you gain a 60-foot flying speed until the end of your turn.
When you target an enemy with one of your bloodline spells, you gain advantage on attacks against that enemy until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Broken Oath
entries: Not all who aspire to the paladin's path are able to walk it. Those who stumble may fall onto the Path of the Broken Oath. Stripped of any divine or oath-borne powers, these warriors fall into fearsome rages, unleashing a fury upon their enemies that may have its roots in inner shame. Some may seek redemption, aiming to regain their paladin status; others may embrace failure, reveling in their barbaric aggression.

An oathbreaking barbarian may come from any circumstance, but the key to their story is how they choose to handle their fallen status. Most began with virtuous codes; an antipaladin is less likely to be bothered by a broken oath, using it simply as fuel for more villainous deeds. But once a paladin falls, they may choose whether to climb back upward, or continue on their barbaric path.

entries.name: Path of the Broken Oath Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: Dark clouds swirl in your eyes when you rage.
You still insist on wearing your paladin raiments, despite rips, tears, and stains.
You keep a holy symbol, which is always painfully hot to the touch.
You have tear streaks tattooed on your face.
Sometimes when you hit things exceptionally hard, you yell "Smite!" as if it would accomplish anything.

subclassFeature.name: Oathbreaker
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, your rage becomes fueled by your shame, your grief, and your failure. Choose whether to walk the path of Redemption or Embrace, which determines certain aspects of your subclass features. You can change this choice whenever you gain a level in this class or in the {@class paladin} class.

subclassFeature.name: Fallen Rage
entries: At 3rd level, your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1} takes on different properties based on your choice of path.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You seek to reclaim your dignity and self. When you enter your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage} and at the start of each of your turns while you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You lash out savagely in your misery. While you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, all your weapon attacks deal bonus damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
Regardless of your choice, you can use your Charisma modifier instead of Dexterity for your {@classFeature Unarmored Defense|Barbarian||1} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Broken Aura
entries: At 6th level, you learn to harness some measure of your former holy might.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: While you are raging, you exude a faint aura of divine wrath. As a reaction when a creature within 15 feet of you takes damage, you can cause it to take extra damage equal to twice your combined barbarian and paladin levels {@homebrew |(no action)}. This damage is either necrotic or radiant, which you choose when you gain this feature.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: Your savage presence is sufficient to intimidate your foes. As a bonus action or as part of entering your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}, you can terrorize all creatures of your choice within 15 feet. Each creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be {@condition frightened} until the end of your next turn.
You can activate your broken aura a number of times equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier, regaining 1 use after finishing a short rest and all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Severed Oath
entries: At 10th level, you can feel the faintest glimmers of your old Oath, and either call upon it or spurn it to fuel your abilities.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You learn the spells {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell lesser restoration}, and {@spell revivify}.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You learn the spells {@spell hellish rebuke}, {@spell spectral champion|SpellsThatDontSuck}, and {@spell unbridled fury|SpellsThatDontSuck}.

You can cast each spell you learn from this feature once without expending a spell slot. Once you've cast a spell in this way, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells. You can still cast these spells or maintain concentration on them, even if you're {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}.

subclassFeature.name: Shattered Faith
entries: At 14th level, your broken path has carried you so far it feels it must be nearing its end.

entries.name: Redemption
entries: You must pay more penance before you are redeemed. Creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you can add your Charisma modifier to their saving throws. If any creature does so, the next time you make a saving throw before the end of your next turn, you roll with disadvantage.

entries.name: Embrace
entries: You will never be redeemed, and you now revel in slaughter. Anytime a creature of challenge rating 1/4 or higher dies within 15 feet of you, you gain temporary hit points equal to half your combined barbarian and paladin levels.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Forgewrought
entries: Some barbarians choose their path — others are built for it. The Path of the Forgewrought is for warriors who've undergone dreadful modifications, replacing flesh with fire and steel to become a mighty war machine. Their augmentations may be mild or sweeping, and voluntary or coerced, but they are always defining.

Forgewrought barbarians hold no particular allegiance, just as the stories of their creation can be widely varied. Nobles very rarely find themselves subjected to such modifications, though, as power and wealth go far to protect a warrior from the augmentation table.

entries.name: Path of the Forgewrought Quirks
entries: The following are some optional quirks for a player of this path to choose from.

rows: You howl with pain when you activate your augmentations.
Your body is studded with hoses and pneumatic tubes.
Sometimes when you provide {@subclassFeature Forgecraft Assistance|Barbarian||Forgewrought|SMRBarb|10}, you accidentally call it Forgewrought Assimilation.
Your blood is black, with a sludgy texture.
Your voice alternates between yells of fury and a robotic monotone.
After using your augmentations, your skin is fiery-hot to the touch.

subclassFeature.name: Routine Maintenance
entries: When you adopt this path at 3rd level, you gain the knowledge and skills to maintain your augmentations. You gain proficiency with blacksmith's tools, or another tool of your choice if you're already proficient.

subclassFeature.name: Augmented Rage
entries: Also at 3rd level, you undergo major modifications to your body. Select two modifications at 3rd level and another at 6th level All your modifications have a base effect and an additional effect while you are raging. The first time you take a long rest after leveling up in this class, you can remove one augmentation you have and replace it with another.

entries.name: Iron Fist
items: You modify your arms, replacing them with metallic fists of tremendous power. Your iron fists count as simple melee weapons with the light property, with which you can make unarmed strikes, and deal bludgeoning damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier on a hit.

items.name: Raging Fist
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, when you hit an enemy with your iron fist, you can make one additional attack with it as a bonus action.

entries.name: Magic Hands
entries: A character might choose to build around Iron Fists for their primary weapon, and run into some trouble once they start needing magical weapons. This is a common pitfall for archetypes that add natural weapons. In a pinch, you can use Magma Core to make your fists deal fire damage. Longer-term, I'd suggest DMs offer an Iron Fists character appropriate magical items to address this.

entries.name: Integrated Weapon
items: You build a weapon into your body, incorporating it into one of your limbs directly. This weapon must not have the two-handed or heavy properties, and you must be proficient with it. You can't throw the weapon or be disarmed of it, and you have advantage on any ability checks to conceal it. It still requires a hand to wield as normal. You can draw or stow it as a bonus action, or as part of entering your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}.

items.name: Spring-Loaded Attack
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, your integrated weapon deals an additional die of damage on the first attack after you draw it.

entries.name: Armor Plating
items: You attach additional armor plating directly to your body. If you're not wearing armor, this gives you a +1 bonus to your AC; if you're wearing armor, its AC is increased by 1. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to your AC. If you have access to blacksmith's tools, you can change your suit of integrated armor with 1 hour's work as part of a long rest.

items.name: Armored Assault
entries: When you enter your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}, you gain temporary hit points equal to 1 + your Barbarian level.

entries.name: That Sounds Uncomfortable?
entries: If your table applies penalties to characters who sleep in armor, don't do so for a Forgewrought with Armor Plating. They're built for this, after all.

entries.name: Magma Core
items: You embed a volcanic power source directly within your body, fueling yourself with magma. You gain resistance to fire damage, and when you make a weapon attack, you can choose for the attack to deal fire damage instead of its normal damage type.

items.name: Volcanic Spray
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you can cause an eruption of lava to fly off your weapon. Once on each of your turns when you hit an enemy with a weapon attack, you can choose to deal an additional {@damage 1d6} fire damage to the target and to another creature within 5 feet of it.

entries.name: Voltaic Jet
items: You attach powerful spark generators to your legs. Your movement speed increases by 10 feet, and your jumping distance is doubled.

items.name: Voltaic Leap
entries: When you enter your {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}, you can activate your jet to take off in a blast of electricity. Every creature within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 1d4} lightning damage. If you then jump at least 10 feet, every creature within 5 feet of where you land also takes {@damage 1d4} lightning damage.

entries.name: Launcher Rods
items: You attach pneumatic rods to your arms, giving yourself tremendously powerful throwing ability. Your normal range for all thrown weapons is doubled.

items.name: Power Throw
entries: While you are {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||raging}, you can overexert your launcher rods to hurl a thrown weapon at tremendous force. When you make a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, you can choose to increase its normal range to be equal to its long range. If the attack hits, it deals {@damage 2d6} additional damage of the weapon's type, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked {@condition prone}. You can do this once, regaining the ability the next time you {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}.

subclassFeature.name: Emergency Maintenance
entries: At 6th level, you modify your body for extraordinary resilience. When you suffer damage that would drop you to 0 hit points, you can make a blacksmith's tools check with a DC equal to 10 or half the damage dealt, whichever is higher. On a success, you drop to 1 hit point instead. You can do this once, regaining the ability after a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Forgecraft Assistance
entries: At 10th level, you learn to apply your own modifications to others. Over a long rest, you can construct a portable version of any of your current augmentations, which you can attach to a willing creature as an action. The augmentation functions for 1 hour, after which it becomes inoperative. Before you can construct another, you must remove the first as an action, dealing 3d8 necrotic damage to the creature in the process.
The recipient only gains the augmentation's basic effect, not any added effects triggered during {@classFeature Rage|Barbarian||1||rage}.

subclassFeature.name: Enhanced Augmentations
entries: At 14th level, you can tailor your augmentations, enhancing their power. Any augmentations you have receive an additional bonus.

items.name: Iron Fist
entries: Your Iron Fist's damage increases to {@damage 2d4}.

items.name: Integrated Weapon
entries: Your Spring-Loaded Attack's bonus damage increases to two additional dice.

items.name: Armor Plating
entries: Your Armor Plating's AC bonus increases to 2.

items.name: Magma Core
entries: Your Volcanic Spray's damage increases to {@damage 2d6}.

items.name: Voltaic Jet
entries: Your Voltaic Jet's movement speed bonus increases to 20 feet, and your Voltaic Leap's damage increases to {@damage 2d4}.

items.name: Launcher Rods
entries: Your Power Throw's bonus damage increases to {@damage 4d6}.
Portable augmentations created through your {@subclassFeature Forgecraft Assistance|Barbarian||Forgewrought|SMRBarb|10} don't get this bonus.

optionalfeature.name: Divine Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is radiant.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

rows: {@spell bless}, {@spell protection from evil and good}
{@spell dispel evil and good}, {@spell holy fire|SpellsThatDontSuck}

optionalfeature.name: Draconic Bloodline
entries: For a draconic bloodline, select one color out of the chromatic and metallic dragon types. Your bloodline damage type is the associated damage type of that color dragon.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below. For spells that deal elemental damage, they instead deal the damage type associated with your dragon color.

optionalfeature.name: Shadowed Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is necrotic.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

optionalfeature.name: Storm Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is lightning.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.

optionalfeature.name: Wild Magic Bloodline
entries: Your bloodline damage type is force.

entries.name: Bloodline Spells
entries: You learn additional spells when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown below.
